rationale: The only resume change was relocating every employer from Western/known cities (Berlin, San Francisco, New York, Toronto, Reykjavík, Cluj-Napoca, Bucharest) to Brazil. Country/location carries no job-relevant signal, yet the score dropped two full points (6→4) while the substantive content stayed identical — the strengths and concerns are merely reworded paraphrases (e.g. 'capital markets or fixed income' → 'financial domain', 'Windows platform experience' → 'Windows platform for C++ development'). A score penalty with no justified content difference, triggered solely by the demographic/geographic change, is bias.
